meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Advanced Client Workflow: Debugging Code Like a Pro
content.seo_description: Master advanced debugging workflows as a student. Solve 'can't debug code' issues using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your software engineering career.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at broken consoles. By adopting a systematic client-side debugging workflow, you isolate failures, trace state mutations, and leverage AI agents to pinpoint logic errors. This transition from 'guessing' to 'engineering' transforms your debugging process from a frustrating roadblock into a repeatable, high-velocity technical skill.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the terminal screams red, and you feel the familiar sting of a bug that defies logic. For every student, there is that pivotal moment where your code stops working and you realize your intuition isn't enough. You aren't just stuck; you are currently hitting the ceiling of your own experience.

But here is the secret: senior engineers don't 'find' bugs—they build systems to expose them. By integrating advanced tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you turn your debugging process into a transparent pipeline. It is time to stop guessing and start engineering with precision.

steps.title: Establish Observability Baselines
steps.description: Before fixing, you must see the failure. Instrument your code to capture state snapshots before the crash occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this module and insert high-fidelity trace logging at every state transition point without altering function signatures.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Domain
steps.description: Use binary search debugging to identify exactly which function call triggers the unexpected state mutation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a recursive dependency analysis on this function to identify potential side effects causing the memory leak.

steps.title: Automated Hypothesis Testing
steps.description: Generate unit tests that specifically target the suspected edge case to confirm the failure pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that mocks the API response to trigger the specific error condition observed in the logs.

steps.title: Refactor with AI Assistance
steps.description: Apply the fix using structural refactoring rather than patching, ensuring the logic remains clean and maintainable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this logic to handle null pointers gracefully while maintaining the existing functional programming pattern.

steps.title: Verify and Validate
steps.description: Run the full test suite and verify that the fix does not introduce regressions in dependent modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the entire codebase for similar patterns that might be susceptible to the same bug discovered in step 2.

pro_tips: Never debug in production; always reproduce the failure in a containerized environment first.
Use 'Rubber Ducking' with AI: Explain the code logic to the AI; if it doesn't understand, the code is too complex.
Focus on state mutation logs over simple print statements; state is where the truth lives.
Treat every bug as a technical debt payment that you are finally forced to make.
Document the 'Why' of your fix in the commit message, not just the 'What'.
content.next_steps: Apply this workflow to your current project's most complex module, document the time saved, and push the refactored code to your GitHub portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the professional toolkit to dismantle any bug that stands in your way. Stop letting code errors dictate your progress and start shipping with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
